fr:mainmatter.fr:p: Let E be displayed over B. Prove that the total category \widetilde { E ^{ \tilde { \mathsf {o}} } } is { \mathopen {} \left ( \widetilde { E } \right ) \mathclose {}} ^{ \mathsf {op} }, and its projection functor is { \mathopen {} \left ( p _{ E } \right ) \mathclose {}} ^{ \mathsf {op} } : \widetilde { E } ^{ \mathsf {op} } \to B ^{ \mathsf {op} }.

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: The total opposite of a displayed category

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: Let E be displayed over B; we define its total opposite E ^{ \tilde { \mathsf {o}} } displayed over B ^{ \mathsf {op} } as follows:

fr:ol.fr:li: An object of E ^{ \tilde { \mathsf {o}} } _{ x } is given by an object of E _{ x }.

Given f : x \to y \in B ^{ \mathsf {op} }, a displayed morphism \bar {x} \xrightarrow [ f ]{} \bar {y} in E ^{ \tilde { \mathsf {o}} } is given by a displayed morphism \bar {y} \xrightarrow [ f ]{} \bar {x} in E.

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: Warning. Do not confuse this construction with the opposite fibered category, which produces a displayed category over B and not B ^{ \mathsf {op} }.

Let E be displayed over B. Prove that the total category \widetilde { E ^{ \tilde { \mathsf {o}} } } is { \mathopen {} \left ( \widetilde { E } \right ) \mathclose {}} ^{ \mathsf {op} }, and its projection functor is { \mathopen {} \left ( p _{ E } \right ) \mathclose {}} ^{ \mathsf {op} } : \widetilde { E } ^{ \mathsf {op} } \to B ^{ \mathsf {op} }.

Let E be displayed over B, and let f:x \to y \in B. Prove that a morphism \bar {f}: \bar {x} \xrightarrow [ f ]{} \bar {y} is cartesian over f in E if and only if \bar {f}: \bar {y} \xrightarrow [ f ]{} \bar {x} is cocartesian over f in E ^{ \tilde { \mathsf {o}} }.
Prove that a displayed category E is a cartesian fibration over B if and only if E ^{ \tilde { \mathsf {o}} } is a cocartesian fibration over B ^{ \mathsf {op} }.

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: The total category and its projection

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: Note that any displayed category E over B can be viewed as an undisplayed category \widetilde { E } equipped with a projection functor p _{ E } : \widetilde { E } \to B; in this case \widetilde { E } is called the total category of E.

fr:ol.fr:li: An object of \widetilde { E } is given by a pair (x, \bar {x}) where x \in B and \bar {x} \in E _{ x }.

A morphism { (x, \bar {x}) } \xrightarrow {{ }}{ (y, \bar {y}) } in \widetilde { E } is given by a pair (f, \bar {f}) where { x } \xrightarrow {{ f }}{ y } and { \bar {x} } \xrightarrow [ f ]{ \bar {f} }{ \bar {y} } : \bar {x}.

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: The construction of the total category of a displayed category is called the Grothendieck construction.
Prove that the total category \widetilde { \underline { B } } of the fundamental self-indexing is the arrow category B^{ \to }, and the projection is the codomain functor.
